intro: The clearest difference between Bugsnag and Airbrake is lock-in — Bugsnag has medium lock-in (Error monitoring, data exportable), whereas Airbrake has medium lock-in (Error data exportable; integrations portable to Sentry). Beyond that, developer experience — Bugsnag scores 5/5 against Airbrake's 4/5. The stated fit for both tools is similar on paper: Bugsnag is pitched as error monitoring platform for web, mobile, and server apps with stability scores, grouping, and release tracking, while Airbrake is pitched as error monitoring and performance tracking for web and backend apps with real-time alerts, backtrace grouping, and deploy tracking. On the strengths side, Bugsnag is cited for mobile and web apps in production, while Airbrake is cited for prioritizing bug fixes by impact. The honest trade-off: both Bugsnag and Airbrake are the wrong call for pre-launch, Simple projects. Choosing between them should come down to the constraint that actually binds your workflow, not surface-level overlap.
quick_take: Bugsnag and Airbrake both target Crash Reporting; their data-level fit descriptions overlap, so decide based on which fit statement matches your team.
verdict_a: Choose Bugsnag if you need a Crash Reporting tool and developer experience matters more than Airbrake's trade-offs.
verdict_b: Choose Airbrake if you need a Crash Reporting tool and you can work around a lower DX rating (4/5).

faq.a: Both Bugsnag and Airbrake use a freemium model with a free tier, so raw pricing is close on paper. The real cost difference depends on volume, seats, and which paid features you actually need. Check current pricing on each vendor's page before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Bugsnag to Airbrake?
faq.a: Migrating from Bugsnag to Airbrake is feasible when their scopes overlap, but the work is in re-mapping configuration, data models, and integrations. Moving off Bugsnag is harder because of medium lock-in (Error monitoring, data exportable). Plan for a dual-run period so you can validate parity before decommissioning.

On our developer-experience score, Bugsnag is rated 5/5 and Airbrake 4/5, so Bugsnag edges ahead. Scores like this smooth over real differences in docs, CLI feel, and SDK quality, so treat them as a starting point. Spend an afternoon in each before relying on the number.

faq.q: Is Airbrake a good alternative to Bugsnag?
faq.a: Airbrake is a reasonable alternative to Bugsnag when Airbrake fits mobile. If your use case matches Bugsnag's sweet spot — mobile — staying with Bugsnag is probably safer. Bugsnag itself is not meant for pre-launch, Simple projects, which is often where users start shopping for alternatives.
